notes: Shankara's three-clause scoping ('presented itself as a duty, not opposed to righteousness, enjoined by scripture') and Ramanuja's 'immediate and incumbent duty' framing are both honoured in the engineering layer's three-condition narrowing (already-arrived, genuinely role-aligned, materially un-pickup-able). The two readings are not conflated — Shankara's emphasis on naturalness and Ramanuja's emphasis on Vedic activation are preserved in their respective summary_paraphrases in the source pack. The pāpam is correctly translated as 'organisational debt' rather than as moral-failure-language; this is a faithful preservation of the karma-based reading where pāpa is real causal residue, not metaphysical guilt. Engineering vocabulary does not erase Sanskrit: pāpam, svadharma, kīrti, dharma-yuddha all appear in the analog. The varṇa-specificity (kṣatriya battle) is honestly narrowed to the role-bound un-replaceability case. PASS on 7.4 and 6.8.
